title: eryngium × microcephalum
intro: Eryngium × microcephalum, also known as sea holly, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the genus Eryngium, characterized by its unique, thistle-like appearance and blue or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Eryngium × microcephalum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
